linkedin.title: Staying informed today feels less like learning and more like drowning.
linkedin.body:
We are constantly bombarded with information. Most of it is irrelevant to our lives, our work, or our interests. The problem with traditional news isn't just the sheer volume. It is the lack of context. When you consume news that is curated for the masses, you miss the stories that actually impact your specific world.

True information is about quality, not quantity. Personalised news shouldn't be about creating a filter bubble that hides opposing views. It should be about surfacing the most relevant, accurate, and insightful reporting on the topics that matter to you. When the noise is stripped away, you can actually understand the bigger picture.

This is why we built Headlinne. We believe technology should help us navigate the news landscape with clarity rather than adding to the clutter. It is about giving you back your time and providing you with a better understanding of the world around you.
linkedin.cta: If you are ready to change how you consume the news, you can see how we do it at HEADLINNE.com.

instagram.title: Top 5 Things In Tech Today

slides.headline: Apple takes OpenAI to court
slides.explanation: Apple has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI. They claim the startup's new hardware division is built on stolen trade secrets.

slides.headline: Meta drops new AI image tool
slides.explanation: Meta pulled a new AI feature from Instagram this week. The decision follows a wave of criticism from users who did not like how it altered their content.

slides.headline: New details in Apple v. OpenAI
slides.explanation: The lawsuit claims former Apple engineers brought trade secrets with them to OpenAI. The case also names Jony Ive's company, IO Products.

slides.headline: EU warns Meta over app habits
slides.explanation: Regulators in the EU are threatening Meta with fines. They are worried that features like infinite scroll encourage unhealthy, compulsive usage.

slides.headline: Record-breaking day for chip maker
slides.explanation: SK Hynix just had a massive debut on Wall Street, raising over 26 billion dollars. The company is a key supplier for Nvidia and is benefiting from the AI boom.

instagram.caption: It has been a busy week in tech with major legal battles and big market moves. From Apple's lawsuit against OpenAI to EU regulators targeting Meta, here is what you need to know. Swipe through for the full rundown. Read more on HEADLINNE.com.

instagram.title: Top 5 Geopolitics Headlines

slides.headline: The New York Times fights back in legal battle
slides.explanation: The newspaper is countersuing a federal agency. They claim the agency is retaliating against them for political reasons regarding a past discrimination case.

slides.headline: Major win for carmakers in UK emissions case
slides.explanation: A high court judge rejected most claims in a massive lawsuit involving 1.6 million drivers. The case accused manufacturers like Ford and Nissan of using illegal emission devices.

slides.headline: Apple sues OpenAI over stolen secrets
slides.explanation: Apple claims that two former employees worked with OpenAI to steal confidential company information. It is a major legal challenge for the ChatGPT maker.

slides.headline: Unlikely political partnership in Pennsylvania
slides.explanation: Democrat John Fetterman and Republican Dave McCormick are teaming up for a joint fundraising committee. This rare move is raising questions about Fetterman’s future in his party.

slides.headline: EU warns Meta over addictive social media features
slides.explanation: European regulators say Facebook and Instagram use designs that keep users on autopilot. They argue these features break EU rules regarding user safety.

instagram.caption: From legal battles in the courtroom to unexpected alliances in the Senate, there is a lot happening today. We have broken down the five biggest stories you need to know about right now. Swipe through to catch up on the latest headlines. Read more on HEADLINNE.com.
